items.headline: Asia-Pacific markets open higher as Trump comments signal Iran war de-escalation
items.summary: Trump said he decided to back off from his recent threat to order strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure "based on the fact we're negotiating."

items.headline: Oil prices fall, stock futures climb on reports U.S. has proposed a cease-fire to Iran
items.summary: Global oil prices tumbled and U.S. stock futures rose on Tuesday evening following reports that the U.S., via intermediary Pakistan, had sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the conflict in the Middle East, now in its fourth week.

items.headline: Judge presses DOD on why Anthropic was blacklisted: 'That seems a pretty low bar'
items.summary: The Defense Department designated Anthropic as a risk to U.S. national security, the first time an American company had been hit with that designation.

items.headline: OpenAI raises additional money to bring record funding round to $120 billion, CFO tells Cramer
items.summary: The fresh capital brings OpenAI's historic fundraise to $120 billion, exceeding the ChatGPT creator's initial target of $100 billion.

items.headline: Gold prices are steady after heavy sell-off
items.summary: The spot gold price began to settle but remained subdued on Tuesday after a tumultuous start to the week, establishing a level around $4,370 per troy ounce.

items.headline: Venezuela opposition leader María Corina Machado calls for full privatization of oil industry
items.summary: Machado's remarks come as the oil industry remains hesitant to invest in Venezuela after the U.S. ousted former President Nicolas Maduro.

items.headline: U.S. sent Iran 15-point plan to end war, report says; Trump says 'in negotiations right now'
items.summary: "Operation Epic Fury continues unabated" to achieve President Donald Trump's military objectives, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said.

items.headline: Meta must pay $375 million for violating New Mexico law in child exploitation case, jury rules
items.summary: Jury reaches a verdict in New Mexico trial in which the state's attorney general alleged that Meta failed to safeguard its family of apps from child predators.

items.headline: Arm stock pops 6% as CEO Haas issues $15 billion revenue expectation for new chip
items.summary: Arm unveiled its first in-house chip on Tuesday at an event in San Francisco, with Meta as the initial customer.

items.headline: Amazon acquires 'approachable' humanoid maker Fauna Robotics
items.summary: Fauna's first product, called Sprout, is a $50,000 bipedal robot that's 3 feet, 6 inches tall and designed to be "approachable and human-friendly."

items.headline: Elizabeth Warren raises concerns over MrBeast’s teen banking app — and its connection to a troubled bank
items.summary: Sen. Elizabeth Warren is raising concerns about a popular YouTuber’s venture into financial services with his acquisition of a banking app called Step and its connection to Evolve Bank & Trust.

items.headline: A bad Treasury auction is offering a glimpse into the anxiety on Wall Street over the Iran war
items.summary: Wall Street jitters about the Iran war spilled over Tuesday into a vital part of U.S. financial markets that typically hum along without a hitch.

items.headline: Software stocks fall as fear of AI disruption is back in full force
items.summary: A new release from Anthropic’s AI agent Claude has revived fears that software will no longer be needed to complete certain tasks.

items.headline: OpenAI shutters short-form video app Sora as company reels in costs
items.summary: OpenAI said it's closing Sora, the short-form video app that went viral after its launch six months ago

items.headline: ‘It feels slimy’: My friend offered to be my adviser, but didn’t tell me he’s paid to push financial products. Can I trust him?
items.summary: “His revenue sharing, which he did not disclose, creates conflicts of interest as advisers may be incentivized to recommend funds that pay them more.

items.headline: Senate Republicans, White House nearing deal to end DHS shutdown amid TSA delays
items.summary: The Department of Homeland Security has been shut down since February over Democratic concerns about its immigration enforcement policies.

items.headline: 3 signals tied to severe market drops are all flashing now
items.summary: Investors who are anxious about the struggling stock market amid the Iran conflict have good reason to worry, as they’re contending with all three of the primary causes of particularly bad years since 1928, according to DataTrek Research.

items.headline: Iran is testing a selective Strait of Hormuz strategy that could deliver another shock to oil markets
items.summary: Iran appears to have adopted a “calibrated strategy” in the Strait of Hormuz — allowing only certain vessels to pass through the crucial waterway.

items.headline: U.S. stocks are looking cheap for the first time in a year

items.headline: Baltimore is first U.S. city to sue over Grok deepfake porn as legal pressure mounts on Musk's xAI
items.summary: Following international regulatory probes, lawsuits are piling up in the U.S. against Elon Musk's xAI and its Grok chatbot.

items.headline: Databricks enters cybersecurity market with Lakewatch launch, bulking up ahead of IPO
items.summary: Databricks wants to draw on AI to help organizations respond to a higher speed of attacks, based on newly disclosed vulnerabilities.

items.headline: Long airport lines may soon disappear as senators sound hopeful on funding deal
items.summary: There is hope that Americans soon won’t have to deal with long lines at many airports around the country, as senators in Washington, D.C., are sounding upbeat about a funding plan that would end a long-running partial government shutdown and restart paychecks for Transportation Security Administration agents.

items.headline: Investors hold $4.8 trillion in these retirement funds—how to choose one for yourself

items.headline: Pentagon reportedly plans to deploy 3,000 troops to the Middle East, lifting oil prices by nearly 5%
items.summary: Oil futures climbed by nearly 5% Tuesday, with global prices settling above $104 a barrel following a report that the Pentagon plans to deploy an airborne Army unit to the Middle East to support its operations against Iran — signaling that the situation in the region is poised to intensify.

items.headline: Kuwait says Strait of Hormuz closure is beyond catastrophic, will trigger domino effect across global economy
items.summary: Iran is holding the world economy hostage by blockading the Strait of Hormuz, said Sheikh Nawaf al-Sabah, the CEO of Kuwait Petroleum Corp.

items.headline: Micron stock sinks for a fourth straight day despite dominant earnings report
